RAILROAD MONOPOLY IGNORES THE LAW

JOSEPH n. CALL, IN SPECIAL
ARTICLE, POINTS OUT MA
TlONAl IMPORTANCE OF

STEAMSHIP LINE ON PA
ClFlC.

pages.raw_text:
Editors Note-Through it's cor
respondent in Los Angeles, The
Daily Press has secured from
Joseph H. Call, who far 10 years.
from 1888 to 1907, was special
counsel for the government and
special assistant attorney general
in the prosecution of land grant,
rate and commerce cases, two
articles, prepared by Mr. Call, ex-
clusively for this newspaper, on
the national Importance of the pro-
posed government steamship line
from the isthmus of Panama to Pa
cific coast ports.


Mr.. Call, through whose efforts
alone 100000 acres of land, orig
inally granted by the government
to railroads as subsidy and improp-
erly handled by the railroads, were
restored to the federal domain, is
recognized throughout the country
as an authority on railroad mat
tiers. He presents some startling
facts and figures to disclose how
and to what extent the railroads
have secured monopoly of trans
portation in the United States, and
to SOW how present conditions
would be remedied by the creating
of competition through the pro-
posed line of government owned
steamships.
